info.title: B2W Estimate API
info.description:
General Information

The EstAPI framework is a .NET Core based architecture to support one or more RESTful Web API endpoints to interact with B2W Estimate.

All controllers in the framework conform to the .NET ApiController behavior and support HTTP verb-based endpoints augmented with attribute-based request routing.

Every endpoint is asynchronous in nature and returns an instance of IActionResult. Depending on the intent of an endpoint, it will either return just a status code, or a status code with relevant data.

No endpoint allows exceptions, if any, to “leak” to the .NET CLR. All exceptions which are propagated by the endpoints are centrally caught, reported, and handled. Exception information is logged to a log file, located in the Logs folder within the deployment folder.

The following sections provide useful information regarding configuration, and usage/testing guidelines. Click on the section title to expand/collapse that section. When you hover the mouse over a section, if the cursor changes to a pointer (a hand with a pointing-finger), you may expand/collapse that section.

Post-Installation Step

The EstAPI application installer configures the application to run in the context of Network Service account, and to access the Estimate database using Integrated Security. This requires the Network Service account to be granted access to the database.

Run the commands in the ConfigureDB file while connected to the Estimate database using an account that has sysadmin rights.

Configuration

Once installed, EstAPI may be configured using its configuration file appsettings.json. This is not typically necessary: the installer sets appropriate values. Caution must be exercised in editing the configuration file, as errors may lead to application misbehavior.

Following configuration values are relevant:

  • Settings
    • RequireTLS
      • When set to true: The application will NOT take traffic on HTTP, but only on HTTPS.
      • When set to false: The application will take traffic on HTTP. This is the default.
    • PageSize
      Maximum number of items that will be returned for each invocation of a GET request to retrieve data. The default is 100.
    • MaxContentLength
      Maximum size (in bytes) of the body content for HTTP requests. The default is 10 MB.

Usage Guidelines

All CRUD endpoints are secured using Bearer Token authorization. The token is expected to be passed using the HTTP Authorization request header, and is retrieved using the Login endpoint.

Optional Client ID Security authorization is also available. When enabled in the API, a ClientID and ClientSecret are expected to be passed in the HTTP headers and must match the values set in the API. The API's values are specified during installation and are stored in the appsettings.json file.

At any point in the interaction with EstAPI, a HTTP status code of 401 in the response indicates that the bearer token may be invalid or, if Client ID Security is enabled, that the ClientID or ClientSecret may be invalid. Typically, a new Login attempt may resolve this issue if it's caused by an invalid bearer token.

  • At the beginning of each "session", invoke the Login endpoint with a valid B2W Estimate user-name and a password.
  • The user-name and password correspond to the Windows Account Name for a user in the Active Directory domain who has requisite access to the CRUD operation.

On successful login, the response will contain a JWT, which contains the AccessToken. This AccessToken must be sent with all other endpoints through the HTTP request authorization header. The RefreshToken can be ignored, and is included for future functionality.

In order to successfully update an entity using a PUT request, you need to first get the entity (based on its key), update relevant fields, and then PUT the entire entity. If you do not perform the update this way, you may unintentionally delete values from non-specified fields.

The AntiTamperToken is data which is returned as part of a GET request. When performing a PUT (update) request, the AntiTamperToken must be sent with the same value. This is to ensure that properties which are provided in the GET request for informational purposes will not be updated since they are meant to be read-only. One example of such a property would be ObjectID, which is useful for referring to a specific record but which cannot be allowed to be changed.

The following are not currently supported with the EstAPI:

  • DELETE is not supported.
  • Cost structure can not be created/updated/modified in an estimate.
  • Container components can not be created/updated/modified in an estimate or resources.
  • Creating/updating organizations in an estimate is not supported.

Testing Guidelines

To facilitate and ease stand-alone testing of EstAPI, a series of Web API requests have been created in Postman and is available as a saved Postman collection file. This file can be found at EstAPI Postman Collection

Postman setup

  1. Download the EstAPI Postman Collection.
  2. Install Postman, if not already installed.
  3. Start Postman, and import the downloaded file using the File > Import option.
  4. Select the Collections tab, and you should see the EstAPI collection.
  5. Click on the ellipsis button in the EstAPI collection, and click on the Edit option in the popup:
  6. Select the Variables tab, and set the Initial Value and Current Value for the following variables:
      BaseURL - HTTP URL where EstAPI is available
      ConnectionString - Connection string to Estimate database
      UserName - Windows Account Name of a valid Active Directory domain user of Estimate in the following format: domain\username
      Password - Password associated with the user
  7. Click on the Save button.

The sample Postman collection uses a pre-request script that automatically sets the AccessToken when sending the request. As long as the variables noted above are specified, there is no need to manually retrieve and save the AccessToken when using the sample collection.

Basic Tests

  1. App-Up Test
    1. Expand the EstAPI collection, and click on the Ping request:
      Click the Send button
    2. You should see a response which is whatever you specified as the last segment in the URL for the request (for example, "Hello").
    3. If you don’t get the success response (HTTP status code 200), verify the following:
      1. IIS is running.
      2. The IIS application pool EstAPI is running.
      3. The URL is correct.
      Do not proceed further unless you can get a success response.
  2. Login Test
    1. Expand the EstAPI collection, click on the Login request, and click on the Headers tab:
      1. Set the value for the userName key to the Windows Account Name of a valid Active Directory domain user of Estimate.
      2. Set the value for the password key to the password associated with the user.
      3. Click the Send button
    2. If the login was successful, the response will contain a JWT which in turn contains the AccessToken:
    3. If you don’t get the success response (HTTP status code 200):
      1. Navigate to Login section.
      2. Click on Login to expand the section.
      3. Click on the GET /Login request for failure scenarios and status codes.

Functional Tests

It is recommended that the result of each test (enumerated in following sections) not only be verified with the expected HTTP response code, but also by using the application itself.

The following applications are therefore recommended:

  1. Postman to carry out the tests.
  2. Estimate.
  3. Browser connected to the Estimate API documentation (this one).

Troubleshooting Guidelines

The following guidelines can be referenced when troubleshooting the EstAPI:

  • Verify correct BaseURL used in request.
  • Verify correct ConnectionString used in request.
  • Verify IIS running.
  • Verify the IIS application pool EstAPI is running.
  • If receiving error: Login failed for user 'NT AUTHORITY\NETWORK SERVICE'. Verify 'NT AUTHORITY\NETWORK SERVICE' account has db_owner access to the correct B2W Estimate database. See Post-Installation Step.

API Documentation

The following sections provide detailed information regarding every functional area. Click on the section title to expand/collapse that section.

get.summary: Get a paginated collection of a model (mapped from an entity).

parameters.name: queryOptions
parameters.example:
$orderby=Name asc
      $skip=4&$top=2
      $filter=startswith(Name, 'C')
      $filter=contains(Name, 'Contractor')
      $filter=EstimateREF eq 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 and Name eq 'Subcontractor'
      $filter=EstimateREF eq 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
      $orderby=Name&$select=Name

      The query options may be combined to create compound queries.
